\[
u(t)=t^3
\]
produces exactly the right-hand side provided, so this is the solution.

u(x) - 0.1813469579287852 * ∫_-1.1508025669664157^9.746297944109074 t + x + 1 * u(t) dt = \left(x + 1\right) \sin{\left(80 \pi x \right)}

Provide the solution u(x).
raw_response:
SOLUTION:  
\[
u(x)= (x+1)\sin(80\pi x)\;+\;0.1813469579287852\Bigl((x+1)A+B\Bigr),
\]
where
\[
A=\int_a^b u(t)\,dt,\qquad B=\int_a^b t\,u(t)\,dt,\qquad 
a=-1.1508025669664157,\quad b=9.746297944109074.
\]

Let
\[
f(x)=(x+1)\sin(80\pi x),\qquad \lambda=0.1813469579287852,\qquad k=80\pi.
\]
Define the ADOMIAN/Fredholm components by
\[
u(x)=\sum_{n=0}^\infty u_n(x),\qquad
u_0(x)=f(x),\qquad
u_{n+1}(x)=\lambda\int_a^b (x+t+1)u_n(t)\,dt.
\]

Then the first four components are:
\[
u_0(x)=(x+1)\sin(80\pi x),
\]
\[
u_1(x)=\lambda\Bigl((x+1)A_0+B_0\Bigr),
\]
\[
u_2(x)=\lambda\Bigl((x+1)A_1+B_1\Bigr),
\]
\[
u_3(x)=\lambda\Bigl((x+1)A_2+B_2\Bigr),
\]
with
\[
A_n=\int_a^b u_n(t)\,dt,\qquad B_n=\int_a^b t\,u_n(t)\,dt.
\]

Because the kernel is separable and rank-2, the Neumann/ADOMIAN series can be summed exactly.  
Let
\[
p=\int_a^b (t+1)\sin(80\pi t)\,dt,\qquad
q=\int_a^b t(t+1)\sin(80\pi t)\,dt,
\]
and
\[
M=\lambda
\begin{pmatrix}
\int_a^b (t+1)\,dt & \int_a^b 1\,dt\\[4pt]
\int_a^b t(t+1)\,dt & \int_a^b t\,dt
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
Then
\[
\binom{A}{B}=(I-M)^{-1}\binom{p}{q},
\]
so the exact closed-form solution is
\[
u(x)= (x+1)\sin(80\pi x)+\lambda\Bigl((x+1),\,1\Bigr)(I-M)^{-1}\binom{p}{q}.
\]

\[

\]
